content: Orthopedic outpatient clinic simulation from a teaching hospital in Tehran. Data from 357 patient records was used to calibrate the simulation. Entities: novice residents (2), experienced residents (1), senior staff physicians, administrative staff. Flow: registration -> cash payment -> physician examination -> paramedic services. Arrival pattern: exponential distribution with normal-distributed intervals, mean 4.2 minutes, SD 5.8 minutes. Metrics tracked: total waiting times across three physician levels and weighted mean waiting times across service stations. Ten scenarios were tested, varying resident/physician counts, clinic start time, and admission schedule. The best scenario (Scenario 9, combining physician work time changes with adjusted admission times) reduced patient wait time by 73.09%.
provenance_note: Paper is about an ORTHOPEDIC outpatient clinic, not ENT. Labeled Adjacent because the structural pattern is directly transferable — same specialty outpatient pattern: patient registration, multiple physician experience levels, exam-based flow, scenario testing of resident/attending counts and schedules. Activity sequence and resource classes translate directly to ENT. Numerical parameters (arrival rates, service time distributions) would need ENT-specific calibration from clinical data.
labels: orthopedic, adjacent, tehran, teaching-hospital, specialty-outpatient, pmc

content: Information aggregated from multiple search-engine snippets describing the IEEE conference paper 'A simulation case study: Reducing outpatient waiting time of otolaryngology care services using VBA'. Model location: Ear, Nose, and Throat outpatient clinic at the University of Illinois Medical Center. Simulation software: Arena with Arena VBA (Visual Basic for Applications). Research question: how does appointment schedule design affect patient waiting time at an ENT clinic? Key finding: an alternative appointment schedule significantly reduces patient waiting times without adding resources. Secondary finding: adding one resident doctor to the system achieved a 5.29% reduction in waiting time. Technique: VBA allowed programmatic exploration of multiple appointment schedules and their interaction with waiting times. The paper itself is paywalled at IEEE and was not directly fetched during this walkthrough; this content was accumulated from search engine result pages that described the paper.
provenance_note: The source URL points to the IEEE paper but the paper itself is paywalled and was never successfully fetched. All content here was aggregated from multiple search engine result snippets ABOUT the paper rather than extracted from the paper directly. Uses the search_snippet_aggregation acquisition method (added to the schema specifically to capture this case — see gap 8 in the README). Flagged for human follow-up via institutional subscription to the IEEE library.
labels: ieee, paywalled, ent, uic, arena-vba, second-party, snippet-aggregated
